scope: Kernel-local evidence labels for source, decision-card, consumption-boundary, evidence-workspace, and Elementor V4 documentation coverage integrity.

labels.meaning: Claim has an official Elementor Help source reference.
Claim has an official Elementor developer documentation source reference.
Claim is backed by a CSS specification reference.
Claim is backed by MDN reference documentation.
Claim comes from an educational or workbook-derived seed only.
Claim is supported only by Kernel-local controlled fixtures.
A current official source was not found or is not enough for a hard claim.
Official docs do not prove the target WordPress project has the feature enabled.
No browser/runtime evidence has validated the behavior.
No Builder execution evidence has been provided.
Official Elementor V4 documentation is represented as context, not as a direct element decision card.
Official docs document Atomic/V4 element discovery or identity boundaries.
Official docs document local class behavior; target class inventory still requires project evidence.
Official docs document global/reusable class behavior; target class inventory still requires project evidence.
Official docs document class priority/order/cascade behavior.
Official docs document Class Manager behavior.
Class documentation does not approve mutation in a target project without separate evidence.
Current user role or permission must be proven separately before target use.
Admin-level permission is documented as a prerequisite or boundary and must be separately proven.
Elementor Pro is documented as a prerequisite or boundary and must be separately proven.
Official docs document V4 element state styling context such as hover, focus, active, and selected states.
Official docs document Variables as reusable design values; target variables and values remain unproven.
Official docs document logical properties such as start/end for writing-direction-aware layout context.
Official docs document V4 attribute add/delete context; target mutation and runtime DOM output remain unproven.
Dynamic tag documentation does not prove target data-source availability.
Official docs document responsive inheritance/editor behavior.
Responsive documentation does not prove browser/runtime behavior.
Reset behavior requires source reconciliation before hard claims.
Official docs document nested clickable/link restrictions.
Official docs or cards do not prove exact UI path in a target environment.
Official docs document a control-level or property-level limitation.
The source has a documented quality note such as typo-like, stale-looking, or conflicting content.
Official docs document V3/V4 hybrid or coexistence context.
Official docs document differences between Editor V3 and V4.
